channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Michaels lifts Western Michigan to 3-2 2OT Frozen Four semifinal win over defending champion Denver
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — Owen Michaels scored his second goal 26 seconds into the second overtime and Western Michigan beat defending champion Denver 3-2 on Thursday night to advance to its first Frozen Four title game.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Venezuelan NWSL star opts out of national team duty fearing possible return issues
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — Portland Thorns forward Deyna Castellanos says she did not play for Venezuela's national team in a pair of recent games because of concerns she might not be able to return to the United States.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: MLS will give further consideration to adopting a fall-to-spring calendar
item.description: Major League Soccer's Board of Governors will give further consideration to adopting a fall-to-spring calendar, a major move that would align the league with its international counterparts.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Masters whiz kid: Jose Luis Ballester takes a different kind of relief and does No. 1 at No. 13
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — U.S. Amateur champion Jose Luis Ballester had to take a different kind of relief Thursday at the Masters, just not the kind found in the Rules of Golf.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Scottie Scheffler begins his pursuit of a third green jacket with a 68 at the Masters
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — Just a couple of weeks ago, Scottie Scheffler thought his game was not quite as sharp as he would have wanted it. “Sharp” might be a poor word choice given what happened in December.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Soccer player dies at 21 after collision with opponent during match
item.description: A soccer player in Peru died this week at the age of 21 after rupturing an artery during a nasty collision with an opponent during a match.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fred Couples shoots 71 at the Masters. So much for the 65-year-old thinking he no longer belongs
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — Fred Couples was so unflappable during the first round of the Masters that not even a sound engineer for one of the broadcasts who wandered down the middle of the fairway as the 1992 champion was trying to tee off threw him out of sync.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Islanders minor league player to miss the remainder of the season after shoving referee into the boards
item.description: Adam Beckman has played his final game of the AHL season after he was hit with a 10-game suspension for "physical abuse of an official." The Bridgeport Islanders have just 5 games left.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congrats to Beth - the Beat Big Will Bracket Challenge Winner
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — The final buzzer has sounded. The confetti has fallen. The Florida Gators are National Champions. KREX Sports Anchor Big Will Levenson was in the building to watch his Alma Mater Florida rise to the top of the College Basketball World. "Greatest experience of my life.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Kvaratskhelia's wonder goal helps PSG beat Aston Villa 3-1 in 1st leg of CL quarterfinals
item.description: PARIS (AP) — A wonder goal from Khvicha Kvaratskhelia upstaged a brilliant curling shot from teammate Désiré Doué as Paris Saint-Germain beat Aston Villa 3-1 in the first leg of the Champions League quarterfinals on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JEROME PUGMIRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bulls favored to land Nikola Jokic if traded, odds maker says
item.description: If the Denver Nuggets were to trade Nikola Jokic, the Chicago Bulls are the top landing spot according to Bookies.com's Adam Thompson, who gave the Bulls a +500 odds to acquire the 3-time NBA MVP.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Jaylon Johnson wants to see real accountability before he says the Bears have changed for the better
item.description: Jaylon Johnson is heading into his sixth NFL season and third go-around riding Chicago's head coaching carousel, and is excited to see the positive change that has eluded them since he joined the franchise, and is looking forward to the Bears' fresh start with new head coach Ben Johnson.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Loyola University Chicago sent preservation notice tied to federally indicted ex-Michigan coach
item.description: Loyola University Chicago has received a preservation notice from attorneys representing a former student-athlete, compelling the school to preserve evidence related to the indictment of former Michigan football assistant coach Matt Weiss on unauthorized computer access and identity theft charges.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Salt Lake Bees celebrate first home game at new Daybreak ballpark
item.description: The Bees will welcome the Reno Aces for a six-game homestand starting April 8, which will include giveaways, music, and activities for the whole family.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Future uncertain for Duke basketball star due to new Trump administration visa policy
item.description: A new visa policy announced by the Trump administration last weekend could impact South Sudanese passport holders, including Duke basketball phenom Khaman Maluach.
item.dc:creator: Keaton Eberly, WGN 9

item.title: Walter Clayton Jr.'s defensive stop gives Florida its 3rd national title with 65-63 win over Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Florida’s Walter Clayton Jr. came up with the perfect going-away present for that spirit-crushing Houston defense that bullied, battered and bedeviled him all night. It was a defensive gem of his own. Right before the buzzer. For the win and the national title.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Retzlaff poised for another big year at BYU
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Last season, BYU quarterback Jake Retzlaff threw for almost 3,000 yards, 20 touchdowns, while rushing for over 400 yards and six TD's as the Cougars shocked everyone with an 11-2 season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Trump honors Dodgers' 2024 World Series championship team
item.description: (KTLA) -- President Donald Trump welcomed Shohei Ohtani, Mookie Betts and the reigning champion Los Angeles Dodgers to the White House on Monday to congratulate them for winning the World Series last season. "That is a very good-looking group of people," Trump said as he began his remarks.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: San Diego senior women's basketball team seeks to compete overseas
item.description: Ten San Diego women over 60 are representing the US in the 17th World MaxiBasketball Championship in Switzerland.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Vara, Fox 5

item.title: The Masters: When it starts, how to watch, betting odds for golf's first major of 2025
item.description: The Masters began Thursday at Augusta National with Justin Rose shooting a 7-under 65 to sit atop a star-studded leaderboard.
item.dc:creator: Dave Skretta, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bears' Jaquan Brisker feels 'best I ever felt,' is eager to test Ben Johnson's offense
item.description: Jaquan Brisker is recovering from a concussion and is eager to face Ben Johnson's offense as the Bears begin their offseason workouts, with a focus on exercise and cognitive performance.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: Defending national champion South Carolina returns to women's title game with 74-57 rout of Texas
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Dawn Staley has never lost a national championship game in three trips as South Carolina's coach. Part of the key to winning those decisive games? Forgetting about what happened the last time she was there. And this time, that was only a year ago.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, WGN 9
